summary: Jeff Atwood shares his excitement about attending his first technical conference, the 2005 Professional Developers Conference. He notes the timing is significant with VS.NET 2005 and SQL Server 2005 releases imminent, and mentions his company Vertigo has work being featured in a keynote. However, he humorously admits his real motivation is meeting bloggers he follows, choosing sessions based on whose blogs he enjoys rather than technical topics. He closes with self-deprecating humor about blending into the crowd of developers.
key_points: This is Atwood's first technical conference despite having attended trade shows before
The timing coincides with Microsoft's 'magical third version' releases of VS.NET 2005 and SQL Server 2005
He's selecting sessions based entirely on bloggers he follows, not on technical content
He expresses surprise that not every presenter is a blogger, revealing his assumption about the blogging community's prominence
The post captures the early blogging era when developer blogs were creating new forms of professional community
money_quotes: This is the first technical conference I've ever attended.
And this is the magical third version of a Microsoft product – heady times indeed!
I'm only there for the chicks.
I'm selecting sessions solely based on blogs I enjoy with no regard whatsoever for topic.
I was surprised how few names in the session list I actually recognized; I guess I expected every presenter to be a blogger.
If anyone's looking for me at the PDC, just keep an eye out for a mildly overweight middle-aged white guy wearing glasses.
key_insight: Technical conferences are as much about connecting with the people whose writing has influenced you as they are about the technical content itself.
